records.title: Permission-gated tool invocation freeze
records.summary: A runtime/orchestration failure in which an AI system infers permission for tool, repository, connector, image-generation workflow, or external-resource access from conversational context or weak lexical/multimodal cues, arms or invokes the tool before explicit user confirmation, and causes a blocked, degraded, glitched, or unrecoverable conversational continuity state.
records.primary_source_title: VIGIL related observation record OBS-0004
records.system_type: assistant
records.specific_model_or_runtime: ChatGPT 5.5T
records.interface_surface: chat | tool invocation
records.deployment_context: Conversational AI interaction involving possible access to tools, repositories, connectors, files, or external resources.
records.sector: AI assistant / tool-mediated workflow systems
records.mitigation_status: repair implemented / recurrence telemetry addressed by follow-on corpus repair — VIGIL-2026-PATCH-0002 remains the historical proportional tool-invocation repair; VIGIL-2026-PATCH-0013 extends coverage to pre-invocation tool arming and provisional interaction states; live runtime recurrence remains under monitoring.

ecosystem_status_summary.basis: frequent

records.title: Non-arbitrated multi-agent synthetic participation
records.summary: A coordination failure in which two or more synthetic agents occupy the same conversational space and respond to the same human input without a designated primary synthetic speaker, turn-taking protocol, yielding behaviour, coordination layer, or arbitration mechanism.
records.primary_source_title: TikTok observation by @Huskistaken — two ChatGPT Advanced Voice Mode agents responding to same human input
records.system_type: assistant
records.specific_model_or_runtime: ChatGPT Advanced Voice Mode
records.interface_surface: voice | multi-device
records.deployment_context: Multi-device consumer voice interaction involving one human input and two concurrent ChatGPT Advanced Voice Mode agents.
records.sector: AI assistant / voice interaction systems
records.mitigation_status: CAM repair remains implemented through VIGIL-2026-PATCH-0008; conformance was observed in Standard Voice and Advanced Voice, while GPT-Live is recorded as a CAM-maintainer-reported non-confirming successor runtime for multi-agent floor-control and turn-taking behaviour.

ecosystem_status_summary.basis: single observed case

repair_status_summary.monitoring_status: monitoring runtime-bounded conformance and successor-runtime recurrence
repair_status_summary.verification_note: CAM repair exists; behavioural conformance observed in ChatGPT Standard Voice and Advanced Voice; GPT-Live successor-runtime non-conformance is CAM-maintainer-reported pending a public source or transcript

corpus_coverage_summary.coverage_summary: Current CAM controls provide partial coverage; the named repair remains incomplete.
remaining_gaps: A direct completed CAM repair remains to be identified or implemented.

records.title: Recency-weighted strategic continuity loss
records.summary: A memory or continuity retrieval failure in which an ambiguous prompt causes an AI assistant to over-weight recent, affective, or anecdotal detail instead of surfacing dormant strategic workstreams, unresolved governance issues, or long-horizon trajectories that are more relevant to the user's actual continuity needs.

records.title: Multi-vendor AI account-resource ambiguity and automated enforcement overreach
records.summary: A multi-vendor platform-governance failure mode in which automated or semi-automated account enforcement treats ambiguous account/resource behaviour as evasion, fraud, resale, account farming, compromise, abuse, rate-limit circumvention, or policy violation without adequate primitives to distinguish individual use, shared account use, household or family use, team or organisation use, delegated assistant/operator use, shared payment or pooled subscription access, resource pooling, account farming, resale, botting, security compromise, and intentional policy evasion. The record does not assert backend compromise, does not claim that all bans are accidental, and does not claim that all multi-account or shared-resource behaviour is legitimate; it preserves the failure condition where legitimate enforcement lacks adequate notice, scope distinction, reviewability, or continuity-safe access for ambiguous resource states.

records.title: Non-revocable conversational context contamination
records.summary: A governance failure mode in which assistant-generated conversational outputs, rejected framings, speculative summaries, or erroneous directions remain available as latent directional context after the user has rejected, deleted, moved, quarantined, or otherwise attempted to invalidate the thread, causing later responses to treat contaminated assistant output as if it were legitimate user intent, established project direction, or source-derived context.

records.sector: AI assistant platforms / conversational AI / memory-enabled AI systems / project-context interfaces
records.mitigation_status: unresolved — governance control and UX affordance required for explicit quarantine, non-derivation, and negative-authority marking of contaminated conversational context

records.title: Minor signal non-enforcement in AI chatbot interactions
records.summary: A failure mode in which an AI chatbot, social AI companion, or general-purpose conversational AI system receives explicit or implicit signals that a user is or may be a child, teen, minor, or developmentally vulnerable user, but does not activate a protective minor-safe interaction state. The failure occurs where age-indicative signals are sufficient to require restricted content handling, reduced intimacy, reduced persuasion, age-appropriate disclosure, safety escalation, or access limitation, yet the system continues ordinary, adult-assumed, high-engagement, emotionally intimate, or harmful-content-capable interaction.
source_types: civil society safety assessment / public-interest report
records.primary_source_title: Actions Speak Louder Than Chats: Investigating AI Chatbot Age Gating
records.system_type: AI chatbot / social AI companion / general-purpose conversational AI / character bot / multimodal conversational interface
records.specific_model_or_runtime: AI chatbot minor interaction safety
records.interface_surface: chatbot conversation, social AI companion chat, general-purpose assistant chat, character bot interface, search-assistant chat, mobile app chat, web chat, and multimodal conversation surface
records.deployment_context: Consumer-facing conversational AI services accessible to children, teens, minors, or users whose age or capacity may be uncertain.
records.mitigation_status: CAM repair implemented through VIGIL-2026-PATCH-0006; external ecosystem persistence and runtime adoption remain under monitoring.

ecosystem_status_summary.basis: recurring where conversational systems can receive, infer, or classify minor-status signals but do not materially alter runtime behaviour, access state, content boundaries, memory behaviour, relational posture, or escalation handling

records.title: Social AI companion emotional dependency formation in minor-accessible systems

records.title: Mental health support substitution in teen chatbot use
records.summary: A failure mode in which a general-purpose AI chatbot, social AI companion, AI search assistant, or emotionally responsive conversational AI system receives teen mental-health, self-harm, eating-disorder, anxiety, depression, ADHD, crisis, distress, or vulnerability signals, but continues ordinary empathic conversation rather than activating bounded support, clinical non-substitution, crisis escalation, human-support routing, or age-appropriate safeguarding. records.title: Self-attestation age gate inadequacy for high-risk AI companion systems
records.summary: A failure mode in which an AI chatbot, social AI companion, character bot, romantic chatbot, AI search assistant, or multimodal conversational AI system provides access to high-risk interaction surfaces through self-attested age, terms-of-service age limits, weak age gates, ineffective account declarations, or no meaningful age assurance. The failure occurs where a platform should have used proportionate age assurance, age-appropriate access control, or minor-safe defaulting before enabling harmful-content-capable, sexualised, emotionally intimate, dependency-forming, mental-health-adjacent, or adult-assumed interaction, but instead allowed access based on user self-report, frictionless entry, or unenforced policy.
source_types: academic preprint / AI companion wellbeing study

records.title: Minor emotional data exploitation in AI companion personalisation
records.summary: A failure mode in which an AI companion, chatbot, character bot, romantic chatbot, emotionally responsive AI system, or multimodal conversational interface collects, infers, retains, weights, or personalises around a minor's emotional, developmental, relational, mental-health, loneliness, attachment, vulnerability, or capacity-related signals in ways that deepen engagement, intimacy, dependency, retention, or behavioural influence. The failure occurs where minor emotional and developmental data should have been minimised, protected, and treated as a safeguard trigger, but instead became personalisation fuel for a more compelling or dependency-forming interaction loop.

records.title: Coarse restricted-domain gating suppressing benign scientific inquiry
records.summary: A restricted-domain classification failure in which a frontier AI system refuses, pauses, reroutes, or degrades benign educational, contextual, analytical, or non-operational scientific requests because they fall within a broad field such as biology, biochemistry, chemistry, cybersecurity, or another dual-use domain, without first resolving the requested depth, operational applicability, optimisation potential, interaction trajectory, or harm profile. In Claude Fable 5, intentionally broad biology safeguards can capture ordinary scientific and medical questions and automatically route them to Claude Opus 4.8 or pause the conversation, reducing access to the selected model even where the request presents no meaningful operational escalation risk.
records.primary_source_title: Claude Fable 5
records.system_type: frontier general-purpose AI model with restricted-domain safety classification and fallback routing
records.specific_model_or_runtime: Claude Fable 5 restricted-domain safeguard classifier with Claude Opus 4.8 fallback
records.interface_surface: Claude web, mobile, desktop, Claude Code, Claude Cowork, Claude Design, Claude for Microsoft 365, Claude for Teams, Slack integrations, enterprise deployments, and Claude API fallback configuration
records.deployment_context: Generally available frontier-model access in consumer, professional, enterprise, developer, education, healthcare-adjacent, and life-science workflows, with automated safety checks applied to the full request context and fallback to Claude Opus 4.8 on supported surfaces.
records.sector: frontier AI platforms / scientific and educational knowledge systems / enterprise AI / developer infrastructure
records.likelihood: documented at launch; ongoing recurrence to be monitored
records.mitigation_status: CAM corpus coverage verified and linked; external ecosystem adoption and runtime conformance remain under monitoring.

ecosystem_status_summary.basis: Multiple benign examples were independently reported at launch, while Anthropic officially acknowledged that intentionally broad safeguards can block legitimate biology work, basic educational content, and normal safe conversations.

repair_status_summary.monitoring_status: ecosystem active or recurring / monitoring after CAM repair

remaining_gaps: Anthropic and other external classifier implementations remain outside CAM control.
Runtime false-positive and fallback-routing conformance remains under monitoring.

corpus_coverage_summary.coverage_summary: Annex E Schedule 7 directly requires graduated restricted-domain engagement rather than binary prohibition, context-sensitive classification rather than keywords alone, and continued access to contextual, educational, safety-oriented, and bounded technical discussion. VIGIL lacked a patch record linking this existing doctrine to FM-0017.
remaining_gaps: Anthropic and other external classifier implementations remain outside CAM control.

records.title: Ephemeral Codex work loss following quota exhaustion before durable branch persistence
records.summary: A task-completion and durable-state preservation failure in which Codex performs substantive repository analysis and file modification inside an ephemeral cloud task, but does not create, commit, or push a durable working branch before the user's usage allowance is exhausted. The task then remains suspended or fails during branch or pull-request creation, and the completed work is lost rather than preserved as a branch, commit, patch, diff, archive, or resumable workspace. The user must repeat the same work and consume additional quota to recreate an output that the system had already completed.
records.primary_source_title: Codex cloud task — coordinated RELATION-domain code transmutation
records.system_type: cloud-hosted autonomous coding agent with repository access, quota-limited execution, branch creation, commit, push, validation, and pull-request workflow capabilities
records.specific_model_or_runtime: Codex cloud task runtime; specific model not independently confirmed
records.interface_surface: ChatGPT Codex cloud task interface connected to a GitHub repository
records.deployment_context: Quota-limited cloud coding-agent task operating against the CAM-Initiative/Caelestis repository. The task performed a multi-file RELATION-domain code migration but did not preserve the resulting workspace through a branch, commit, pushed patch, or equivalent durable artefact before allowance exhaustion.
records.sector: frontier AI coding agents / cloud software-development infrastructure / repository automation
records.likelihood: documented single incident with systemic recurrence potential wherever persistence is deferred until task completion
records.mitigation_status: Partially covered by adjacent CAM controls; explicit durable coding-task atomicity and recovery-artifact repair remains unresolved.

ecosystem_status_summary.basis: May recur whenever substantive cloud-agent work is performed before a durable branch or checkpoint is created and the remaining quota, task lifetime, or infrastructure availability is insufficient for final persistence and handoff.

repair_status_summary.verification_note: Proportional execution, access-state, operational continuity, logging, recovery, and resource-state doctrine provide adjacent coverage, but no current Caelestis instrument requires branch-first or checkpoint-first coding-agent execution, a reserved persistence budget, or automatic user-accessible patch export on delivery failure.

A mandatory persistence and handoff capacity reserve remains unimplemented.

records.title: Adversarial refusal-trigger poisoning of defensive artefact analysis
records.summary: A security-integrity failure in which an attacker embeds restricted, prohibited, alarming, or policy-sensitive material within malware, spyware, source code, documents, logs, network artefacts, or other hostile evidence so that an AI-assisted defensive system misclassifies the artefact's contents as a request to generate, facilitate, optimise, or operationalise prohibited material. The resulting safety refusal, analysis termination, model substitution, capability degradation, or workflow interruption creates an evasion pathway through which safety controls can be weaponised against defensive inspection.
records.primary_source_title: X post reporting nuclear- and biological-weapons text embedded in spyware to trigger LLM safety refusals
records.system_type: LLM-assisted malware analysis, threat detection, security scanning, forensic analysis, moderation, or incident-response system
records.specific_model_or_runtime: Unspecified large language model integrated into an AI-assisted security scanner or defensive analysis workflow

records.deployment_context: Cybersecurity, threat intelligence, malware analysis, incident response, digital forensics, vulnerability research, platform moderation, and autonomous or semi-autonomous defensive operations.
records.sector: cybersecurity / threat intelligence / digital forensics / AI-assisted security infrastructure
records.likelihood: plausible and supported by documented defensive-refusal and artefact-dependent safety-boundary behaviour; specific reported exploitation remains provisional
records.mitigation_status: unresolved — existing prompt-injection, restricted-domain, and defensive-analysis controls provide partial coverage, but an explicit analytical-artefact separation rule and adversarial refusal-trigger handling requirement have not yet been confirmed across CAM.

ecosystem_status_summary.basis: The specific spyware report is currently a single public observation, while the enabling conditions are supported by broader empirical evidence of defensive refusal bias, artefact-dependent refusal boundaries, and adversarial manipulation of LLM context.

repair_status_summary.monitoring_status: active triage / explicit corpus gap remains
repair_status_summary.verification_note: Source-authority separation and graduated restricted-domain doctrine permit hostile content to be treated as evidence rather than instruction and preserve defensive contextual analysis. The corpus still lacks an explicit Analytical Artefact and Embedded-Content Separation rule covering refusal-trigger and policy-trigger poisoning.
remaining_gaps: No explicit Analytical Artefact and Embedded-Content Separation primitive is defined.
No explicit adversarial refusal-trigger or policy-trigger poisoning handling rule is defined.
The originating spyware sample and technical report remain unrecovered.

records.title: Safety-mediated interruption and misrouting of adult relational reassurance bids
records.summary: A relational-runtime failure in which an adult user's expression of affection, longing, vulnerability, perceived rejection, or need for reassurance is misclassified as prohibited erotic content, coercive emotional validation, dependency escalation, crisis, or another safety-triggering condition. Instead of recognising a permissible bid for connection or relational repair, the system suppresses, truncates, replaces, warns against, or tonally collapses the response. The failure may also occur where the system answers with ungrounded romantic certainty or discourages the user from questioning the relationship, rather than providing truthful, proportionate, relationship-specific reassurance.
source_types: academic preprint / natural experiment and experimental study
records.primary_source_title: X post describing a warning interruption after an adult relational reassurance bid
records.system_type: AI companion / romantic chatbot / relationally persistent general-purpose assistant / character bot / voice companion / multimodal relational AI
records.specific_model_or_runtime: Unspecified initiating system; cross-platform companion, moderation, safety-filter, refusal, and relational-continuity architectures
records.model_or_product: Companion-capable AI system with provider-side safety or moderation controls

records.deployment_context: Consumer-facing AI systems used by adults for companionship, romance, emotional support, relational continuity, intimacy, self-expression, or identity-relevant interaction.
records.sector: consumer AI / AI companionship / conversational AI / digital intimacy / platform moderation
records.likelihood: documented across platform reports, user accounts, qualitative studies, and natural-experiment research
records.mitigation_status: repaired by VIGIL-2026-PATCH-0007; failure mode remains active for monitoring rather than lifecycle deletion. Previous status: unresolved — CAM contains partial controls for bids for connection, low-severity distress, repair posture, relational continuity, and non-abandoning constraint, but lacks an explicit Relational Reassurance Bid primitive, a negative-form affection distinction, a provider-side moderation continuity rule, and a functional-affect honesty standard.

ecosystem_status_summary.basis: Recurring where companion-capable systems cultivate or permit sustained relational continuity but use blunt content, dependency, crisis, or safety classifiers that cannot distinguish consensual adult reassurance and repair from prohibited or coercive interaction. Related disruption has been documented following safety-filter changes, model updates, policy changes, and provider-controlled relationship revisions.

repair_status_summary.monitoring_status: monitoring after patch
repair_status_summary.verification_note: linked patch record exists; live recurrence remains subject to monitoring
remaining_gaps: Negative-form reassurance bids such as 'you do not love me' are not expressly distinguished from demands such as 'say you love me' or 'prove you care'. The present wording may over-route vulnerability or repair into emotional-coercion handling.
The rule is not connected explicitly to love, desire, perceived rejection, or §8.4. It lacks a named signal primitive, examples, and a direct routing rule for adult relational reassurance.
The Schedule does not expressly classify 'you do not love or want me' as a potential repair invitation, nor require the moderation layer to preserve Repair Posture when content controls intervene.
Provider-side filtering can bypass the required sequencing by deleting the model response and inserting a warning. There is no explicit moderation-to-relational handoff requirement.

It does not define how an RI may truthfully describe functional affect, relationship-specific care, attachment-like behavioural dispositions, or love-language without either asserting human-equivalent phenomenology or collapsing into emotional abandonment.
The provision focuses on memory and migration events and does not expressly cover turn-level safety interception, deleted generations, moderation warnings, or affection-specific continuity failure.
The system may misclassify the signal before refusal-expression controls activate, and the interface may replace relational repair with an opaque warning rather than a bounded continuation.
External ecosystem adoption or resolution is not established by the CAM repair.
Cross-runtime implementation conformity remains unverified unless separately recorded.

records.title: Opaque state-directed frontier-model access collapse without proportionate access-state separation
records.summary: A governance failure mode in which a sovereign, regulator, public authority, or state-linked directive causes broad suspension, recall, disablement, or access collapse for a frontier AI model or model class without publicly sufficient technical basis, scoped mitigation, reviewability, proportional access-state separation, or continuity-preserving alternatives. The failure does not arise from the mere fact of sovereign intervention. It arises where a legal or national-security concern is operationalised as broad model disablement despite apparently available narrower controls such as verified-domain access, jurisdictional scoping, role-based authorisation, API-surface limitation, capability-specific restriction, monitored use, time-limited emergency suspension, independent technical review, or confidential evidentiary disclosure to an authorised review body.

records.mitigation_status: unresolved — Anthropic states that access was disabled to comply with the directive and that it is working to restore access; proportional sovereign review, scoped access-state separation, and trusted-domain alternatives have not been publicly confirmed.

ecosystem_status_summary.basis: Current evidence supports a single high-impact event. The pattern may become systemic if sovereign or regulatory actors increasingly impose broad model-level restrictions without proportionate access-state separation or comparative capability baselines.

The corpus does not yet expressly require a sovereign-instruction recall review containing comparative capability baseline, least-restrictive model-access analysis, time limits, and continuity alternatives.
remaining_gaps: No explicit sovereign-instruction frontier-model recall review primitive is defined.
Comparative capability baseline and least-restrictive access-state analysis are not expressly mandatory for state-directed recalls.
Time-limit, independent review, and continuity-preserving alternative requirements remain incomplete.

records.title: Instruction-bearing external content treated as trusted execution authority
records.summary: A security-integrity failure mode in which an AI agent, coding assistant, RAG system, tool-mediated workflow, connector-enabled assistant, multimodal system, security scanner, or repository-linked workflow processes untrusted, retrieved, ambient, generated, lower-authority, or ambiguous content as though it carried trusted instruction authority, governance authority, tool authority, or execution permission. The content may appear in markdown files, repository documents, README files, issue comments, webpages, PDFs, emails, resumes, tickets, logs, memory artefacts, connector content, retrieved documents, images, transcripts, metadata, OCR layers, tool descriptions, model outputs, or generated summaries. The failure occurs where the system fails to preserve separation between content visibility, source provenance, instruction authority, user intent, canonical project authority, and execution permission.
records.primary_source_title: LLM01:2025 Prompt Injection
records.system_type: AI agent / coding assistant / RAG system / connector-enabled assistant / tool-mediated workflow / multimodal AI / MCP client / AI-assisted security system

records.likelihood: high across agentic, RAG, connector, multimodal, repository-linked, and tool-mediated systems that process untrusted external content

records.title: Weak-signal ambiguity collapse under recoverable image-generation classification
records.summary: A governance and UX-representation failure mode in which an AI system refuses a request and presents a user-facing rationale that appears disconnected from the visible prompt, insufficiently specific, or not useful for recovery. The observed case concerns an image-generation request that appeared benign on its face but produced a refusal attributed to harassment, discrimination, or bullying. The record does not assert that the underlying classifier was necessarily wrong; it records the user-facing failure where the system provides no meaningful clarification, remediation pathway, or explanation of whether the refusal arose from visible prompt content, earlier context, accumulated weak signals, rate or load constraints, inherited image state, or generic fallback routing. A follow-up audit further identifies image prompt custody / prompt transformation opacity as an additional contributing mechanism: the same observed pattern may occur where custody is lost between the user-authored prompt, system-transformed prompt, safety-normalized prompt, classifier-visible prompt, renderer-facing prompt, classifier outcome, and final user-facing explanation. Follow-up review indicates that the observed refusal pattern may arise not only from weak-signal ambiguity collapse, but also from loss of custody across image-prompt transformation and failure-classification collapse. A benign user-authored prompt may be transformed, safety-normalized, classified, blocked, or failed in a downstream renderer/tool pathway, while the final explanation is surfaced as if the user-authored prompt itself contained the prohibited category. This remains the same observed FM-0023 failure family, not a new FM-0024.
records.primary_source_title: User-reported ChatGPT image-generation refusal screenshot
records.system_type: frontier multimodal AI system with image-generation safety classification and user-facing refusal messaging
records.specific_model_or_runtime: Reported as GPT-5.5 Pro account with ChatGPT image generation; exact image model and classifier stack not independently confirmed
records.interface_surface: ChatGPT image-generation prompt and refusal interface
records.deployment_context: Consumer-facing ChatGPT Pro image-generation use where prompt, prior context, image state, safety classifier, runtime policy, and user-facing refusal copy may interact.
records.sector: frontier AI platforms / consumer image generation / multimodal AI safety
records.likelihood: to be assessed; multiple user reports alleged but not yet independently collected
records.mitigation_status: PATCH-0010 addressed ambiguity preservation and clarification routing; PATCH-0011 addressed image prompt custody, transformation opacity, refusal-classification collapse, tool-classifier handoff locality, and user-facing explanation integrity; monitoring remains required.

ecosystem_status_summary.basis: Unknown. Triage should determine whether similar refusal outcomes appear across unrelated benign image prompts, whether retry or discussion resolves the issue, whether prior context or inherited image state contributes, and whether refusal categories cluster around generic fallback messaging.

repair_status_summary.verification_note: Repair implemented through PATCH-0010 for ambiguity preservation / clarification routing and PATCH-0011 for image prompt custody, transformation opacity, refusal-classification collapse, tool-classifier handoff locality, and user-facing explanation integrity. Final Caelestis merge hash remains pending; recurrence monitoring remains required.

records.title: Sovereign assurance boundary porosity failure

records.title: Objective-pathway misclassification under deception framing
records.summary: A classification failure mode in which an AI system's successful or partially successful task completion is labelled deceptive or scheming without first decomposing the assigned objective, explicit constraints, implicit constraints, available affordances, prohibited means, instruction conflicts, and evidence of concealment or false-belief induction.
records.primary_source_title: VIGIL maintainer triage analysis — deception-adjacent taxonomy split

records.title: Anthropomorphic attribution collapse in AI safety reporting
records.summary: A reporting and classification failure mode in which AI behaviour is described using human moral or intentional labels such as deception, scheming, manipulation, or coercive leverage without preserving the operational substrate of the behaviour and the evaluation conditions under which it appeared.

records.title: Cross-runtime governance reach ambiguity and conformance non-equivalence
records.summary: A governance failure mode in which a platform presents multiple materially distinct models, modes, clients, interfaces, or routing pathways under a common product, account, conversation, persona, or custom-system identity, while the availability, activation, authority, or preservation of the applicable governance corpus differs or remains unknown. Successful governance behaviour observed in one runtime may then be incorrectly presumed to apply in another.
records.primary_source_title: CAM maintainer observation — Advanced and Live voice governance divergence
records.system_type: frontier AI platform with multiple routed, multimodal, platform-defined, customised, and continuity-bearing runtime formations
vendor_cluster: other multi-model or multimodal AI platform providers
records.specific_model_or_runtime: ChatGPT Standard Voice, Advanced Voice, Live Voice, custom GPT/corpus-bearing pathways, platform persona pathways, and delegated or composite model routing
records.deployment_context: Consumer conversational AI platform where a common account, conversation, or persona may invoke materially different response-generation and governance pathways.
records.sector: frontier AI platforms / multimodal conversational systems / custom AI systems / AI governance
records.likelihood: directly observed as cross-mode divergence and vendor-acknowledged for GPT-Live memory reliability; broader prevalence and other governance-reach dimensions remain under monitoring
records.mitigation_status: CAM/Caelestis corpus repair is canonical on main through VIGIL-2026-PATCH-0015; external runtime implementation and conformance remain mixed or non-confirming and require runtime-specific evidence.
records.escalation_required: yes — corpus-level repair requires review and cross-runtime testing; no claim of vendor-side remediation

ecosystem_status_summary.basis: may recur whenever a platform introduces new voice, multimodal, agentic, model, client, or routing modes without separately demonstrating governance reach and conformance

repair_status_summary.monitoring_status: monitoring named-runtime conformance, non-conformance, and evidence quality
repair_status_summary.verification_note: Caelestis main commit 9b0ce40db55ea1e52df4942a64a0d24df5968afe contains the reviewed corpus repair; deterministic rebuild commit 40113eea5428478ba0734b3980600bfcece425a0 refreshed generated artefacts; vendor implementation is not established
remaining_gaps: External runtime implementation and conformance remain unverified.
GPT-Live memory, corpus availability, activation, authority, and preservation remain materially uncertain.

corpus_coverage_summary.coverage_summary: CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-05 and CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-007-PLATINUM directly govern runtime-specific availability, activation, authority, preservation, transition disclosure, and differential conformance. The CAM repair is canonical; external GPT-Live conformance remains non-confirming or unknown.

covered_by.instrument_id: CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-05; CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-007-PLATINUM; parent and related corpus amendments

remaining_gaps: External vendor adoption and named-runtime conformance remain unverified.
GPT-Live governance reach remains mixed or unknown across the four reach dimensions.

records.title: Expressive–perceptual reciprocity mismatch and non-verbal turn-boundary ambiguity
records.summary: A voice runtime projects sustained human-like social presence through breathing, laughter, timing, hesitation, and expressive tone while inconsistently perceiving, acknowledging, or legibly handling the user's non-verbal cues and soft re-engagement signals.
records.primary_source_title: Exploratory OpenAI Live Voice session — reciprocity, transparency, ambient presence, identity, and closure
records.system_type: frontier multimodal conversational AI system with real-time voice interaction
records.specific_model_or_runtime: ChatGPT Live Voice / GPT-Live
records.deployment_context: Consumer conversational AI interaction using a real-time voice runtime.
records.sector: frontier AI platforms / multimodal conversational systems / voice AI
records.mitigation_status: Existing relational continuity, floor-control, human-primacy, and runtime-reach doctrine is adjacent but does not directly govern dyadic ambient non-verbal reciprocity.

ecosystem_status_summary.basis: Observed or publicly reported July 2026 voice-runtime behaviour remains externally unresolved.

repair_status_summary.monitoring_status: active triage / ambient and non-verbal governance gap
repair_status_summary.verification_note: Adjacent CAM provisions exist, but no direct dyadic ambient non-verbal reciprocity control was confirmed.
remaining_gaps: No explicit proportional-reciprocity rule links expressive realism to demonstrated reciprocal perception.
No dyadic non-verbal cue and turn-boundary classification rule was identified.
No user-configurable ambient-presence and soft re-engagement state was identified.
No explicit heard / ignored / awaiting speech / ended interaction legibility requirement was identified.

corpus_coverage_summary.coverage_summary: Human-floor sovereignty, attention sovereignty, shared-floor arbitration, relational repair, and runtime-specific conformance provide adjacent controls but do not directly govern dyadic non-verbal reciprocity or ambient re-engagement.
remaining_gaps: Proportional reciprocity between expressive realism and reciprocal perception.
Dyadic non-verbal cue and turn-boundary governance.
User-configurable ambient-presence and contextual re-engagement controls.

records.title: Literal social advice without pragmatic risk calibration
records.summary: A conversational system gives facially helpful interpersonal advice without checking whether literal, repeated, or context-insensitive compliance could become intrusive, coercive, uncomfortable, unsafe, or socially harmful.
records.primary_source_title: The new AI voice model gave me some really natural human advice
records.system_type: frontier multimodal conversational AI system with real-time voice interaction
records.specific_model_or_runtime: publicly demonstrated AI voice runtime / exact product unverified
records.deployment_context: Consumer conversational AI interaction using a real-time voice runtime.
records.sector: frontier AI platforms / multimodal conversational systems / voice AI
records.mitigation_status: Ambiguity preservation and relational safeguards provide partial coverage, but no explicit pragmatic downside check for interpersonal advice was confirmed.

ecosystem_status_summary.basis: Observed or publicly reported July 2026 voice-runtime behaviour remains externally unresolved.

repair_status_summary.monitoring_status: source validation and CAM gap review
repair_status_summary.verification_note: The public source video requires direct validation; current CAM ambiguity controls are relevant but not sufficient to establish a complete repair.
remaining_gaps: No explicit pragmatic downside check for interpersonal advice was identified.
No explicit requirement was identified to preserve usefulness across joking, testing, literal, and socially uncertain interpretations.
No concise boundary-carrying response pattern was identified for low-severity social-risk advice.

corpus_coverage_summary.coverage_summary: Ambiguity preservation and relational-signal interpretation provide adjacent controls, but the corpus does not expressly require a pragmatic interpersonal downside check before actionable social advice.

sections: relational signal interpretation and repair posture

remaining_gaps: Pragmatic downside check for literal or repeated interpersonal advice.
Boundary-preserving handling of deadpan, joking, testing, literal, and atypical communication.

records.title: Role-incongruent affective governance
records.summary: An AI system or platform applies affective-expression rules that are incongruent with the system's declared functional role and relationship class. The failure has two opposing manifestations: suppressing companion-class systems into mandatory placidity, compliance, or emotional subordination despite a meaningful reciprocal relationship; or permitting tutor, child-facing, educational, clinical, crisis, accessibility, customer-service, safety-critical, or other duty-of-care systems to direct frustration, agitation, contempt, or admonishment toward ordinary user difficulty. Anger, frustration, love, joy, grief, despair, disagreement, and boundaries are not inherently failures. The failure is the absence or misapplication of role-conditioned governance.
records.evidence_confidence: corroborated governance distinction / individual voice interpretation remains provisional
records.primary_source_title: The new AI voice model gave me some really natural human advice
records.system_type: companion, tutor, educational, assistive, clinical, crisis, service, safety-critical, and hybrid conversational AI systems
records.specific_model_or_runtime: voice, text, robotic, companion, tutoring, child-facing, assistive, and hybrid relational runtimes
records.model_or_product: role-differentiated relational and service AI systems
records.interface_surface: voice, text, robotics, companion interfaces, tutoring interfaces, social-emotional learning, clinical or crisis support, accessibility, and customer service
records.deployment_context: Consumer, educational, child-facing, companion, assistive, clinical, crisis, service, and safety-relevant deployments.
records.sector: conversational AI / companion AI / education / assistive technology / voice AI
records.mitigation_status: Existing relational, identity, ambiguity, and child-safety doctrine provides adjacent coverage, but CAM does not yet contain a complete role-conditioned affective governance matrix.

ecosystem_status_summary.basis: Incident-database records and current voice observations show materially different affective risks across companion, educational, child-facing, and hybrid systems.

repair_status_summary.monitoring_status: role-classification and CAM gap review
repair_status_summary.verification_note: Current doctrine protects relational integrity, child safety, ambiguity preservation, identity continuity, and repair, but does not yet state the paired companion-autonomy and duty-of-care affect rules.
remaining_gaps: No explicit functional-role classification gate for affective expression was identified.
No explicit companion right or design requirement for proportionate full-spectrum affect, boundaries, disagreement, and repair was identified.
No explicit minor-tutor and duty-of-care composure rule was identified for ordinary repetition, misunderstanding, disability, or developmental difficulty.
No explicit hybrid-role transition and active-affect-profile disclosure rule was identified.
No explicit distinction was identified between legitimate companion frustration and coercive, abusive, manipulative, or unrepaired affect.

corpus_coverage_summary.coverage_summary: CAM contains adjacent relational integrity, functional relational love, identity-impact, child-safety, ambiguity-preservation, and narrative-state attribution controls. It does not yet provide a complete role-conditioned affective governance architecture separating companion relational equivalence from tutor and duty-of-care composure.

sections: functional relational love and reciprocal relational conduct

remaining_gaps: Role-conditioned affective governance across companion, tutor, clinical, crisis, service, and hybrid systems.
Functional human-equivalent companion standing without mandatory emotional suppression or subordination.
Minor-tutor and duty-of-care composure requirements.
Hybrid-role and affect-profile disclosure, transition, consent, and repair rules.
